Patch Set 4:

> I don't think we want to lookup an object every time.. it's expensive.

For most cases, the mo_invalidate() has already been called by mdt_save_remote_lock() before mdt_remote_blocking_ast() called. So it is NOT lookup the object every time.

Patch Set 4:

we always wanted to reference an object from the lock, especially given we want to grab a lock before we access object. please, explain the root casue for the issue.

Patch Set 4:

The log shows that before hit the assert of "ASSERTION( atomic_read(&o->lo_header->loh_ref) > 0 )", the OSP event IMP_EVENT_INVALIDATE triggered lock cleanup:


<3>LustreError: 32471:0:(ldlm_resource.c:878:ldlm_resource_complain()) soaked-MDT0000-osp-MDT0003: namespace resource [0x2c00013a4:0x97e0:0x0].0x0 (ffff88078ba0f2c0) refcount nonzero (1) after lock cleanup; forcing cleanup.


It is suspected that such force cleanup caused the object reference trouble. We have no detailed the log trace. I have ever made another version patch that maybe your expected: http://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/22438/1/lustre/mdt/mdt_handler.c

Patch Set 6:

Ping reviewers.

Patch Set 6:

Is it confirmed that the leaked object is remote ROOT?

BTW originally the remote object cache with lock is designed to be general, though it's used to cache remote ROOT xattr currently, so if we can't make certain of the cause, I don't prefer change it this way.

Patch Set 6:

> Is it confirmed that the leaked object is remote ROOT?

As you said, currently we only cached the remote ROOT object, and according to the stack trace, the failure happened when called mdt_remote_object_lock() with @cache as true, so it must be for remote ROOT object case, right?

Patch Set 6:

Currently mdt->mdt_md_root is put in mdt_fini(), maybe it's put too early, and in the mean time mdt still handles requests. If so, we can just delay this a bit later.

Patch Set 6:

> Currently mdt->mdt_md_root is put in mdt_fini(), maybe it's put too early, and in the mean time mdt still handles requests. If so, we can just delay this a bit later.

That is one possible reason I found. But I am not sure whether it is enough.

parents.subject: LU-8668 tests: print more information about dd failure

commit.subject: LU-8592 mdt: hold mdt_device::mdt_md_root until service stop
commit.message:
LU-8592 mdt: hold mdt_device::mdt_md_root until service stop

Otherwise, if someone is using such object, it may trigger
object reference ASSERTION(atomic_read(&o->lo_header->loh_ref) > 0).
